context.projectOverview: Adds a single stack-aware bober-security-auditor role driving one runSecurityAudit() core, consumed by an in-pipeline fail-closed gate that vetoes sprint-passed on critical findings/timeout/parse-failure and a standalone `bober security-audit` CLI with a configurable blocking threshold. Findings reuse the locked ReviewResult shape, persist to .bober/security/, and flow into the priority hub; an opt-in deterministic scanner pre-filter (with slither and semgrep parsers) grounds the LLM judgment. Implements arch-20260712-security-audit-agent-team (ADR-1..6) for users whose generated code manages real customer funds.

completedSprints.title: Security config section, audit types, .bober/security store
completedSprints.summary: SecuritySectionSchema opt-in default-off; security-audit-types.ts (VulnClass/SecurityFinding/SecurityAuditResult/deriveVerdict); security-audit-state.ts store. Commits f76ee2e/fc20eae/4ae188f.

completedSprints.title: bober-security-auditor agent + runSecurityAudit core + stack knowledge
completedSprints.summary: security-auditor-agent.ts: runSecurityAudit(contract, evaluation|null, projectRoot, config, priors?) — fail-closed parse (parsed:false forces 'blocked'), curator-role read-only tools (NO bash), provider errors reject, persists via saveSecurityAudit internally. stack-knowledge.ts resolveStackSecurityContext. agents/bober-security-auditor.md. Commits 0990156/ddf27bc/e5cf267/40c1488.

completedSprints.title: Fail-closed SecurityAuditGate in the pipeline
completedSprints.summary: src/orchestrator/security-gate.ts: evaluateSecurityGate(...) -> SecurityGateVerdict {blocked, reason: disabled|clean|critical-finding|timeout|audit-error, result?} — Promise.race time-box (config.security.timeoutMs default 300000), never throws, parsed:false checked before verdict (elevates to audit-error), best-effort saveSecurityAudit guard never flips verdict; exports renderSecurityFeedback (pure, '[CRITICAL] vulnClass: desc at path:line', cap 20). pipeline.ts additive 98+/0-: gate under config.security?.enabled===true at top of if(evaluation.passed) BEFORE updateContractStatus('passed'); on block appends security-audit-blocked event + feeds findings via pendingSecurityFeedback->evalFeedbackParts (same channel as evaluator feedback) + mirrors eval-failed retry tail (continue / needs-rework return); on clean appends security-audit-clean and falls through; code-review+documenter never run on block. Commit e60422c. Suite 3980. Advisories (low): cap-20 magic number duplicated pipeline.ts:488 vs unexported MAX_RENDERED_FINDINGS; needs-rework return shape divergence :516 vs :692; narrowing cast as SecurityFinding[].

contract.title: Add the standalone `bober security-audit` CLI with configurable blocking threshold
contract.description: The on-demand profound audit: a Commander command `bober security-audit [target]` that synthesizes a lightweight contract descriptor from the target path (or working tree), runs runSecurityAudit with evaluation=null, persists the artifact, prints a findings summary, and exits with a CI-friendly code driven by security.standaloneBlockOn ('critical' default, or 'important' to also fail on important-bucket findings). The pipeline gate's critical-only veto is untouched.

successCriteria.description: `bober security-audit [target]` is registered following the existing CLI command-module pattern; it runs runSecurityAudit(descriptor, null, projectRoot, config) where the descriptor is synthesized from the target path (or the working tree when omitted) with a timestamped descriptor id (never colliding with pipeline contractIds), and persists the artifact via saveSecurityAudit.

Exit-code semantics: with default standaloneBlockOn 'critical', the process exit code is 2 iff review.critical is non-empty, else 0; with standaloneBlockOn 'important', exit 2 also when only important-bucket findings exist; an audit error / parsed:false exits 2 (fail-closed) with the error on stderr.

The command prints a human-readable summary (verdict, counts per bucket, top findings with path:line, artifact path) and works when config.security is absent by falling back to the schema defaults for the audit run (standalone mode does not require enabled:true — the CLI invocation IS the opt-in).
The pipeline gate's veto logic is unaffected: security-gate tests still pass unchanged, and no gate code path reads standaloneBlockOn.
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass; ESLint zero errors; full suite green. The built dist CLI registers the command (help output lists security-audit).

nonGoals: Do not require security.enabled=true for the standalone command (the explicit invocation is consent); do NOT let this looseness leak into the gate.
Do not implement scanners or hub emission here (sprints 5-6) — but do not preclude them: the command calls the same core.
Do not audit remote URLs — target is a local path only.
Do not add a skill wrapper yet (sprint 7).
stopConditions: All five criteria verified; exit codes proven by test at the command-action level (injected exit/process boundary per repo CLI test convention).
contract.definitionOfDone: An operator can run `bober security-audit` (optionally scoped to a target path) against any local codebase under any configured provider, get a cited findings summary plus a persisted .bober/security/ artifact, and wire the exit code into CI with a configurable severity threshold.
assumptions: The CLI uses Commander with per-command registration modules (precedent: src/cli/commands/research.ts registered in src/cli/index.ts) and keeps clock/process.exit at the .action boundary so core logic stays testable.

standaloneBlockOn and the full SecuritySectionSchema already exist from sprint 1; when config.security is absent the CLI constructs the section via SecuritySectionSchema.parse({}) to obtain defaults for the run.

A 'descriptor' is whatever minimal SprintContract-shaped object runSecurityAudit needs for its prompt (id/title/description/scope hint from the target path) — sprint 2's core signature accepts it; if the core needs a narrower input type, tighten the core's parameter type rather than fabricating fake contract fields.
Auditing the working tree means the auditor's read-only tools explore projectRoot (or the target subpath) — no git-diff scoping is required for the first cut.
outOfScope: Scanner pre-filter execution (sprint 5 — flows in automatically once the core gains priors), hub emission (sprint 6), skill wrapper and docs (sprint 7).
contract.generatorNotes: Anchors may have drifted — re-locate by pattern. (1) Mirror src/cli/commands/research.ts: a registerSecurityAuditCommand(program) exporting from src/cli/commands/security-audit.ts, wired in src/cli/index.ts next to the other registrations; .action stays thin — parse config, build descriptor, call an exported runStandaloneSecurityAudit(deps) that returns {result, exitCode} so tests never spawn a process. (2) Descriptor id: `security-audit-<ISO-ish timestamp slug>` — timestamp taken at the .action boundary (repo convention: no Date.now() in core, inject the clock). (3) Exit codes: 0 pass, 2 blocked-by-threshold or fail-closed error — reserve 1 for Commander's own usage errors; set process.exitCode rather than calling process.exit() mid-flow if that matches existing commands. (4) Threshold: pure exported thresholdVerdict(review, standaloneBlockOn) → boolean; keep it in the CLI module (NOT the gate) so sc-4-4 is structural. (5) Summary printing: follow the logger/chalk style of existing commands; include the .bober/security/ artifact path. (6) Tests: fake runSecurityAudit via dependency injection; table-test the threshold matrix (critical-only config × {critical, important-only, clean, parse-fail} results).
contract.evaluatorNotes: For sc-4-2: demand the full threshold matrix as tests — {standaloneBlockOn: critical|important} × {critical findings, important-only, clean, parsed:false, core throws} = assert exit code for all ten cells. For sc-4-3: run with a config object lacking the security key and assert the audit still executes with defaults (spy on the injected core to inspect the section it received). For sc-4-4: grep standaloneBlockOn usages — must appear only in CLI module + schema + tests, never in security-gate.ts or pipeline.ts. For sc-4-5: after npm run build, execute `node dist/cli/index.js security-audit --help` (built-dist smoke, the lesson from the medical sax bug) and confirm it prints usage without crashing. Full suite green.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
